---
title: "Research"
description: "Vern Glaser's research program — algorithmic organizing and cultural entrepreneurship — studied through qualitative and computational methods."
---

```{=html}
<header class="page-banner banner--research">
  <h1>Research</h1>
  <p class="page-banner__sub">Algorithmic organizing — how algorithms, AI, and analytics reshape organizations.</p>
</header>
```

::: {.lead}
I study the question, *How do organizations strategically change practices and culture?* Most of my research involves understanding how organizations use algorithms, arguments, and analogies to change routines and to create new capabilities.
:::

## Algorithmic Organizing

Most of my work follows algorithms, AI, and data analytics as they become woven into organizational routines, decisions, and strategy. Rather than treating an algorithm as a self-contained tool, I theorize algorithms as *entangled, relational, emergent, and nested assemblages* — of humans, algorithms, data, and artifacts — and trace how they evolve as they move across contexts and over time.

This thread runs through [*The Biography of an Algorithm*](https://doi.org/10.1177/26317877211004609) (winner of the 2021 James G. March Prize), the [*Organizations as Algorithms*](https://doi.org/10.1111/joms.13033) metaphor for management theory, and work on how algorithmic routines can produce [*dynamic inertia*](https://doi.org/10.1111/joms.12819) — the way organizations using algorithms to adapt can, paradoxically, fail to keep pace with their environment. More recent work asks what happens to organizational **values** as AI agents take on real work, including [*Reading* The Technological Society](https://doi.org/10.5465/amr.2021.0159) on the mechanization of values and an [assemblage perspective on hybrid human–AI agency](https://doi.org/10.5465/amr.2024.0126).

I edited the volume *Algorithmic Organizing* (Research in the Sociology of Organizations, 2025) and convene the IDeaS workshops on interpretive and ethnographic approaches to data science.

::: {.band--wash}
### Methods: Qualitative + Computational

I primarily use qualitative research methods, and have conducted studies in the predictive analytics and online display advertising industries. I have also conducted ethnographic work with an entrepreneurial start-up in the analytics industry.

This fieldwork is increasingly paired with computational text analysis — including [topic modeling for management research](https://doi.org/10.5465/annals.2017.0099) — and, most recently, generative AI as a research partner ([*Chatty Actors*](https://doi.org/10.1177/10564926231219622); [*Robotic Artistry*](https://doi.org/10.1177/14761270261448648)). I have also written on [finding theory–method fit](https://doi.org/10.1177/1056492617706029) across qualitative approaches to theory building.
:::

## Cultural Entrepreneurship

A second thread examines how entrepreneurs and organizations make new categories, markets, and practices *legitimate* — through framing, analogy, and the design of cultural artifacts. This includes work on [stretching financial-market analogies](https://doi.org/10.1287/orsc.2016.1069) to make online advertising exchanges work, [goal-based categorization](https://doi.org/10.1177/0170840619883368) in the display advertising industry, and how organizations [inscribe artifacts to change routines](https://doi.org/10.5465/amj.2014.0842) (*Design Performances*). In *Designing Legitimacy*, Michael Lounsbury and I draw on design studies to expand the scope of cultural entrepreneurship.

::: {.eyebrow}
Theoretical topics of interest
:::

::: {.topics}
[Algorithms and analytics]{.topic}
[Framing, arguments, and analogies]{.topic}
[Routines and capabilities]{.topic}
[Strategic management]{.topic}
[Strategy-as-Practice]{.topic}
[Organizational theory]{.topic}
[Culture and social cognition]{.topic}
[Family business]{.topic}
[Institutional logics]{.topic}
[Technology]{.topic}
[Innovation and Entrepreneurship]{.topic}
:::

<hr class="rule">

The full record — every article with its abstract and DOI — is on the [Publications](publications.qmd) page.
